channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: A house fire cost Patrick and Susan everything, including their two dogs
item.description: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.
item.dc:creator: Becc Bird and Josh Brine

media:group.media:description: The couple's dogs, Mercy and Oscar, died as a result of the fire.

item.title: Has the PM squandered his 'Big Tobacco' moment with gambling ads?
item.description: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

media:group.media:description: The NRL is seen as one of three 'big gorillas' blocking gambling reform.

item.title: Video of 4m tiger shark beheading prompts call for change
item.description: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.
item.dc:creator: Will Corbett

media:group.media:description: An expert has called for limits to the size of sharks that can be legally caught in WA.

item.title: From colonisation to 'safe space': The story of Dhirri-aay-aay
item.description: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.
item.dc:creator: Brigitte Murphy

media:group.media:description: Aunty Polly Cutmore shares the emotional connection and history behind her traditional lands.

item.title: After her husband's death, Annie discovered he had placed 100 bets a day
item.description: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.
item.dc:creator: Elise Kinsella

media:group.media:description: Ms Ashton has been campaigning for stronger gambling regulations since her husband's death.

item.title: Dance helped stroke survivor Laura find confidence in her new body
item.description: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.
item.dc:creator: Selina Bryan

media:group.media:description: Laura Della Pasqua (L), MADE (Mature artists Dance Experience) Dance Participant at the Moonah Arts Centre in Hobart on 14 May, 2026.

item.title: Whether they're faded, frayed or stained, every hat tells a story
item.description: For many, hats are more than a way to keep the sun off your face; they tell stories and hold memories from far and wide. Five men share their collections.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Johnson

media:group.media:description: Joe Caruana stacks his hats together in his shed.

item.title: 'Grossly incompetent': Action taken against rogue MP after online outburst
item.description: Contempt proceedings have been launched against Mark Latham after he attacked a tribunal that found he unlawfully vilified and sexually harassed another state MP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Lewis

media:group.media:description: Mark Latham attacked the NSW Civil and Administrative Tribunal in a series of social media posts.

item.title: First memorial to Australia's deadliest Vietnam War battle unveiled
item.description: The longest and deadliest battle for Australian troops in the Vietnam War has finally been memorialised with a statue to the Battle of Coral–Balmoral erected in Gundagai.
item.dc:creator: Jess Scully

media:group.media:description: A statue depicting Richard Norden VC drag-carrying his section commander Joe Kelly back from enemy lines.

item.title: Is the US-China rivalry over?
item.description: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.
item.dc:creator: Bang Xiao

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have agreed to manage their rivalry rather than let it manage them.

item.title: Rugs on racehorses not acceptable shelter in sweeping welfare changes
item.description: Adequate shelters with a minimum floor size and at least one fixed wall to protect horses from wind and rain are new enforceable requirements for those keeping racing horses in Tasmania, as the state makes sweeping changes to animal welfare standards.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Moran

media:group.media:description: Properties will be inspected in the coming weeks.

item.title: A little box of joy came into my life at just the right time
item.description: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.
item.dc:creator: Virginia Trioli

media:group.media:description: Fionn O'Donoghue livestreams the goings-on of two bluetit birds living in his garden in Ireland.

item.title: 'Cricket is a funny game': Cruel dismissal denies Marsh IPL century
item.description: Mitch Marsh receives a nightmare slice of luck after a stunning innings put him on course for the triple figures.

media:group.media:description: Mitch Marsh was well on his way to a century before being cruelly run out at the non-striker's end.

item.title: Judge declares another mistrial in Harvey Weinstein rape case
item.description: Harvey Weinstein's third trial in New York on a charge that he raped aspiring actress Jessica Mann has ended in a mistrial, after a jury failed to reach a unanimous verdict.

media:group.media:description: Jurors have deadlocked in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial, forcing another mistrial in a #MeToo-era case.

item.title: Aussies climb into podium contention as Vingegaard claims win
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard stamped his authority on the Giro d'Italia, but three Aussies are in the top 10 overall and all remain in consideration for the podium.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jonas Vingegaard is the 115th rider to win stages at all three grand tours.

item.title: Sick of the AFL and all its changes? The SANFL is quietly kicking goals
item.description: SANFL crowds are continuing to climb each year after COVID-19 restrictions put a dent in numbers, but its broadcast reach is winning ratings, and streaming has reached new heights.
item.dc:creator: Malcolm Sutton

media:group.media:description: Sturt ended Glenelg's hopes of three premierships in a row by winning the flag at Adelaide Oval in 2025.

item.title: Man assisting police after death of another man north of Bundaberg
item.description: Emergency services were called to a Gooburrum home after reports that a man in his 30s was unconscious and not breathing.
item.dc:creator: Grace Whiteside

media:group.media:description: A man is assisting police with their enquiries.

item.title: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing
item.description: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

media:group.media:description: US President Donald Trump (left) and Chinese President Xi Jinping meet at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, on Thursday.

item.title: Fracking ban rethink on back-burner — but is it delaying the inevitable?
item.description: The SA government argues South East fracking would help unlock gas reserves at a time of limited supply — but it's a move that's vehemently opposed by many in the local community.
item.dc:creator: Leah MacLennan

media:group.media:description: Opponents of fracking in the SA South East attended a country cabinet meeting in Mount Gambier.

item.title: Voters head to polls for Stafford by-election
item.description: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focussed on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: LNP's Fiona Hammond and Labor's Luke Richmond are vying to be elected as the member for Stafford at Saturday's by-election.

item.title: How a Wallabies winger uses art to connect with his culture
item.description: While Dylan Pietsch is a star on the rugby field for the Wallabies and Western Force, he turns to his love of painting to express himself and be creative.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Dylan Pietsch designed the jersey he and his Wallabies teammates wore against the Lions last year.

item.title: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know
item.description: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Courty

media:group.media:description: Diphtheria is a highly contagious bacterial disease that can infect the nose, throat and airways.

item.title: Migrant workers trained as nurses to alleviate rural SA health shortages
item.description: The rural town of Cleve in SA is getting a much-needed boost to its health system with a pilot program sponsoring five nurses from the Philippines to undertake traineeships to become enrolled nurses.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Bailey

media:group.media:description: Trainee nurses Leaster Mendoza and Trisha Marie at Cleve District Hospital.

item.title: 'Recipe for disaster': Road safety expert 'not surprised' by fatal Qld bus crash
item.description: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.
item.dc:creator: Tara Cassidy, Bryn Wakefield, and Chloe Chomicki

media:group.media:description: The large coach crashed at Gumlu, between Townsville and Bowen, after 4pm on Thursday.

item.title: Big money and a free degree: Athletes choose US college football over AFL
item.description: A new generation of Australian athletes is skipping the MCG entirely, eyeing US college football scholarships instead of Aussie Rules.
item.dc:creator: Bec Symons and Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Mack Brown has researched every university that offers agricultural studies.
